body: Since the inserted Observation JSON body does not match any Data Stream schema that was inserted, the assessment for Part 2 Create/Replace/Delete will always fail. This will probably be the same for Control Streams / Commands.
title: Observation insertion does not match inserted Data Stream schema

body: The data that the assessor attempts to insert to `/datastreams` is not valid according to required components in the schema. Some of these required components come from schemas that should have been recursively retrieved, such as `dataStream_create.json` which specifies a requirement on `{ "$ref": "dataStream.json" },`, which is a reference to a different schema.
title: Data Stream insertion does not use a valid schema

body: The conformance tests for Part 2 will use the root endpoint `/` instead of what was provided `/sensorhub/api`
title: Conformance tests for Part 2 don't keep the correct API root

body:
For example, the `links.json` schema in the `common` schemas does not recursively pull the original definition of `Link` to compare JSON against the actual schema.

This can be resolved by pulling the "master" schema repository that uses accurate relative references to previously written schemas.
title: Schemas are not recursively pulled if they contain a $ref tag

body: The conformance test against Links will always fail when there is no link with `"ref": "self"`, but I cannot find in the schema files where this is required, rather than just an example of a relation.

comments.body: I think it would be more useful to read the schemas and directly map the JSON responses to the requirements in the schema.

title: Conformance to "Links" uses examples as requirements

body: Currently, if you put a URL to a protected CS API endpoint, the assessor will fail to progress to the next page and show a 401 error. However, the next page asks for authentication, so there is no way to get to this next page on protected endpoints.
title: Prompt for authentication on landing page URL

body:
Currently when trying to assess a local development server, an error will popup saying `Private and reserved IP addresses are not allowed`. 

If people want an easy way to debug their servers to comply with OGC API - Connected Systems, then I think this is a critical component.
title: Allow assessments on local development servers
